forecasts.reasoning: A thorough search of news articles and Wikipedia up to April 2026 revealed no evidence of a Member of the European Parliament (MEP) resigning between June 9, 2025, and July 1, 2025. While the Huawei lobbying scandal resulted in investigations, police searches, and immunity waiver requests for several MEPs (such as Giusi Princi, Fulvio Martusciello, and Salvatore De Meo) around May to June 2025, none of them announced their resignation during the specified window. The European Parliament broadly resisted the efforts to lift immunity at that time. The only tangentially related resignation found was that of Jacek Tomczak, who was a Polish deputy minister who resigned in late 2024, not an MEP. Thus, it's highly certain that this resolves as No.

Searches for the exact phrases "reverse purchasing" and "reverse purchase" on nytimes.com return zero results. The specified time window for publication was extremely narrow (June 9 to July 1, 2025), and there is absolutely no evidence that the New York Times published an article using either of these exact phrases during this period or at any other time nearby. Given the tight constraints and lack of any Google index matches for the New York Times, the probability is set to the absolute minimum allowed.

The question resolves Yes if Wikipedia lists all 7 specified countries (China, Iran, Myanmar, North Korea, Russia, Turkmenistan, and Venezuela) as currently blocking X as of early July 2025. Based on news reports from 2025 and 2026, X remained blocked in all of these authoritarian countries. Most significantly, Venezuela’s block, initiated in August 2024 by Nicolás Maduro, was not lifted until January 2026 after his capture, making it a "yearlong ban" that cleanly overlaps with the July 1, 2025 resolution date. Other notoriously censored internet environments—such as China, Iran, North Korea, Myanmar, Russia, and Turkmenistan—also maintained rigid blockades of the platform throughout 2025. Given Wikipedia's typical structure, these ongoing national bans would undoubtedly remain listed in its 'Current' section regarding the censorship of Twitter/X. Accounting for minor edge cases (such as short-lived Wikipedia formatting changes or unacknowledged unblockings), the probability is overwhelmingly high.

President Yamandú Orsi is confirmed to have attended the July 2025 BRICS Summit in Brazil, as shown by numerous news reports of his meetings with global leaders like PM Modi during the event. To satisfy the resolution criteria, an announcement by the government to attend had to be made before July 1, 2025. An article published by Ámbito on June 27, 2025 reports that 'Presidency sources confirmed to Ámbito that Orsi, along with Foreign Minister Mario Lubetkin, will arrive in the Brazilian city on Friday, July 4...' This serves as a public communication on behalf of the government confirming his in-person attendance prior to the deadline. Because he actually attended and his travel plans were publicly detailed and confirmed by the Presidency in late June, the criteria for a YES resolution are fully met.

The evidence is overwhelming that Francis Suarez did not announce a candidacy for Florida governor before July 1, 2025. In the final weeks of June 2025, Suarez was actively focused on municipal legislation to move Miami municipal elections from odd to even years, a move that would have extended his mayoral term into 2026. He signed this measure into law on June 26, 2025. It is highly inconsistent for a politician to actively legislate to extend his mayoral term while simultaneously launching a statewide campaign for governor. Furthermore, reports indicate that when this effort to extend his term was eventually abandoned by September 2025, Suarez formally ruled out a run for governor. He subsequently left office at the end of 2025 and took jobs in the private sector and media (joining Fox News and Nexar's board) in early 2026. Mentions of Suarez in May 2025 polling simply reflect his status as a termed-out mayor and potential candidate, not an officially declared one. There is no record of an announcement before the July 1, 2025 deadline.

The question asks if Casey DeSantis announced her candidacy for Florida governor before July 1, 2025. Numerous highly authoritative and recent sources from mid-2025 to early 2026 confirm that she has not entered the race. An August 2025 Politico report noted she "has not made any firm moves toward a candidacy." A February 2026 Politico article titled "The Casey DeSantis question looming over Florida's governor race" reported that insiders expect she won't run at all and confirmed that she and the governor haven't floated her name to insiders or started fundraising. Polls in July 2025 also characterized her potential run as "hypothetical" and noted that she had yet to declare your candidacy. Consequently, it is virtually certain she did not announce a gubernatorial run before the July 1, 2025 deadline.
